incident.title: BigQuery cost spike — full table scan missing partition filter
incident.stack_trace:
GCP Billing Alert: BigQuery spend exceeded $500 threshold
  Job: order_analytics_query_20240316
  Bytes billed: 193,273,528,321,024 (180 TB)
  at bq_cost_query.build_order_query (line 52)
  No partition filter applied — full table scan

working_context.incident_title: BigQuery cost spike — full table scan missing partition filter

active_hypothesis.description: Based on past incident 'BigQuery full table scan — missing partition filter on order_date': build_order_query accepts start_date and end_date parameters but never uses them in the WHERE clause. The query filters only on status, causing BigQuery to scan the full 180 TB table.

The query filters only on status, causing BigQuery to scan the full 180 TB table.

archival_hits.title: BigQuery full table scan — missing partition filter on order_date
archival_hits.root_cause: build_order_query accepts start_date and end_date parameters but never uses them in the WHERE clause. The query filters only on status, causing BigQuery to scan the full 180 TB table.
archival_hits.fix_pattern: Add WHERE order_date BETWEEN start_date AND end_date before the status filter. Provide safe defaults (last 7 days) so the partition filter is always applied even when no dates are passed.

patch_patterns.title: BigQuery partition filter pattern — always apply date range
patch_patterns.root_cause: Query parameters passed to a function are not propagated into the SQL WHERE clause, resulting in no partition pruning.

file_history.title: BigQuery full table scan — missing partition filter on order_date
file_history.root_cause: build_order_query accepts start_date and end_date parameters but never uses them in the WHERE clause. The query filters only on status, causing BigQuery to scan the full 180 TB table.
file_history.fix_pattern: Add WHERE order_date BETWEEN start_date AND end_date before the status filter. Provide safe defaults (last 7 days) so the partition filter is always applied even when no dates are passed.

change_hints: commit a3f9: removed partition filter default (2h ago)
code_graph.error_function_source:
def build_order_query(
    status: str,
    start_date: str | None = None,
    end_date:   str | None = None,
) -> str:
    """
    Build a SQL query to fetch orders by status.

    BUG: partition filter is never applied even when start_date /
    end_date are provided. The WHERE clause only filters on status,
    causing a full table scan every time.
    """
    # BUG: ignores start_date and end_date entirely
    query = f"""
SELECT
    order_id,
    customer_id,
    order_date,
    status,
    total_amount
FROM
    `project.dataset.orders`
WHERE
    status = '{status}'
"""
    return query.strip()

rag_chunks.code:
def test_estimated_cost_is_high(self):
        """Full table scan on 180 TB table is very expensive."""
        from bugs.bq_cost_query import build_order_query, estimate_scanned_bytes, estimate_cost_usd
        query   = build_order_query("COMPLETED")
        scanned = estimate_scanned_bytes(query)
        cost    = estimate_cost_usd(scanned)
        assert cost > 100, f"Expected cost > $100, got ${cost}"

def test_status_filter_preserved(self):
        """Partition filter must not remove the status filter."""
        from bugs.bq_cost_query_fixed import build_order_query

        query = build_order_query("REFUNDED", "2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")
        assert "REFUNDED" in query
        assert "order_date" in query.lower()

def test_partition_filter_present(self):
        """Fixed query must include order_date partition filter."""
        from bugs.bq_cost_query_fixed import build_order_query

        query = build_order_query("COMPLETED", "2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")
        assert "order_date" in query.lower(), (
            "Fixed query must include order_date partition filter"
        )
        assert "between" in query.lower() or ">=" in query.lower()

def test_query_missing_partition_filter(self):
        """Buggy query has no partition filter — cost constraint violated."""
        from bugs.bq_cost_query import build_order_query, check_cost_constraint

        query  = build_order_query("COMPLETED", "2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")
        result = check_cost_constraint(query)

        # The bug: order_date appears in SELECT but NOT in WHERE as a filter
        # Check that there's no WHERE filter on order_date (no BETWEEN / >= etc.)
        import re
        has_where_filter = bool(re.search(
            r"where.*order_date.*(between|>=|<=|>|<)", query.lower(), re.DOTALL
        ))
        assert not has_where_filter, (
            "Buggy version should NOT filter on order_date in WHERE clause"
        )
        assert result["passed"] is False, "Cost constraint should fail on buggy query"
        assert result["scanned_gb"] > 10

domain_constraints.description: All queries against partitioned tables must include a partition filter on the partition column (order_date). Queries without a partition filter will be rejected.
domain_constraints.rationale: Unfiltered queries on 180 TB table cost ~$900 per execution. Partition filters reduce scan to <10 GB (<$0.05).

domain_constraints.description: Estimated bytes scanned per query must be below 10 GB (10,737,418,240 bytes). Queries exceeding this threshold indicate missing or insufficient partition pruning.
domain_constraints.rationale: Cost governance policy: single analytical query must not exceed $0.05.
